item.dc:creator: deafkitten

item.title: Sunday WHOLESOME dump to bless your day and warm your soul (having a long week? I got you)
item.description:
1. We must protect her at all cost! P.S. Would love to have a friend like that
2. The accidentally flying cat
3. Give me pets human
4. Left and right
5. I like how kitty's OS (CatOS) stop working for a sec
6. Universal language of a worried mom is the same not only across nations but across species too
7. Danger bush
8. PILLOW
9
10. Tail rudder
11
12. It's the little things in life that makes us smile the most
13. Kid's technically correct
14. Wait whot, mate?
15. With this final post, I would like to tell you a little bit about myself and my backstory. My brother and I were born in Eastern Europe and had to live through the 90s. After the collapse of the Iron Curtain, most of the nations under it collapsed too - economies were ruined, people lost jobs, and it took a lot of time to get things going again. I remember times were bad, but for some reason, they didn't feel as bleak because we had games to play, and honestly, that makes me remember my childhood fondly. We played everything - from Super Mario Bros to Excitebike - and had a great time! Now, two decades later, as adults, we want to bring that same feeling to current generations who maybe don't have it easy or just want to escape into a world for a while. That is one of the reasons we started a small dev studio (the other one being our unconditional love for games, although some AAA companies are making it hard these days) and are working on our third game, which you can wishlist here: https://store.steampowered.com/app/2336120/Do_Not_Press_The_Button_To_Delete_The_Multiverse/
item.dc:creator: TheseusGames

item.dc:creator: DumpingSh1tandBeingKind2

item.title: Just venting
item.description: Story time: I live 4 hours away from my family ( mom and sister). 4 of us have birthdays in September ( my mom, 2 of my nephews and myself). My sister decided to organise a "September people bday party" with everyone instead of singular ones. She told me to come as a surprise for my nephews and mom as they all miss me apparently. So I arrived and there were some guests of course. I wish everyone a happy birthday and give them gifts. Came the time for cake. They all made cakes for each others birthday. They also baked a cake for one of the guests, my sister's boyfriend's brother, whose birthday was also in September. I didn't know the guy before but still brought him a gift too. That's when I realised nobody wished me happy birthday all day and there has been no gift and no cake for me. I feel stupid to rant about that at my age but since she thought of her new boyfriend's brother while on the actual day of my birthday I was alone and almost nobody even remembered it, I thought she was going to at least include me with the others.
item.dc:creator: GussDeBlod

item.dc:creator: anonbyanon

item.title: You want me to what?
item.description: I left a lifetime of indoctrination for freedom and sanity. I just spoke with a family member who described evangelism as something we were raised to do. Something they must do. And expressed frustration when the person they were evangelizing didn't accept the faith and how they had to work harder to save the person's soul. I said ok, but forcing religion on someone is really impolite. Pique interests first over rejecting a no. No still means no, and pursuing that is kind of like being an aggressor in an unwanted relationship. They got angry. I apologized because family. I want to be available for a person who was done wrong by their parents to have this way of thinking. And I'm angry at my family for raising children to not be capable of thinking critically and abstractly. A person in this family may be taking in a dependant soon and I don't have the capacity to prevent it even though it should never happen. Twisted theology, and emotional abuse are just the surface. I feel bad about not being able to stop it from continuing.
item.dc:creator: dezriel310

item.dc:creator: fortytwoembers

item.title: Carrots in their rightful place
item.description: Can life be totally rotten when there are little boys putting carrots in their rightful place? "If life seems jolly rotten There's something you've forgotten And that's to laugh and smile and dance and sing When you're feeling in the dumps Don't be silly chumps Just purse your lips and whistle, that's the thing And Always look on the bright side of life Always look on the right side of life" [ Eric Idle, Always Look on the Bright Side of Life, 1979 ] I hope you smile or crack a mental smirk today, from this or an unrelated thing - even if only a brief moment. Cheers!!
item.dc:creator: epicurusaquinas
